result:
Confirmed. Here's the complete inventory of real `decrRefCount()` references in `src/t_string.c` (comment on line 186 excluded):

| Line | Enclosing function | Context |
|---|---|---|
| 128 | `setGenericCommand()` (line 87) | Releases `current_decoded` after comparing decoded current value for `IFEQ`/`IFNE` condition check |
| 207 | `setGenericCommand()` (line 87) | Releases `milliseconds_obj` after using it to rewrite the command with `PXAT` for expire propagation |
| 543 | `getexCommand()` (line 499) | Releases `milliseconds_obj` after rewriting command for expire propagation |
| 894 | `msetexCommand()` (line 821, via shared codepath before `incrDecrCommand` at 901) | Releases `milliseconds_obj` after expire-related command rewrite |
| 1347 | `increxCommand()` (line 1196) | Releases `milliseconds_obj` after expire-related command rewrite |
| 1629 | `lcsCommand()` (line 1420), at `cleanup:` label | `if (obja) decrRefCount(obja);` — releases decoded object `a` |
| 1630 | `lcsCommand()` (line 1420), at `cleanup:` label | `if (objb) decrRefCount(objb);` — releases decoded object `b` |

Total: **7 real calls**, all found via `grep -n "decrRefCount" src/t_string.c` then manually filtering out the comment at line 186 ("...will call `decrRefCount()` at the end of `call()`..."). Note: CodeGraph's `callers` command truncates output at 20 results and, being sorted by file path, cut off before reaching `t_string.c` — so grep + manual verification was used as ground truth here.
